meta.name: bicep-code-best-practices
meta.title: Bicep Code Best Practices Skill
meta.description: Infrastructure as Code with Bicep Triggers on: **/*.bicep

original_definition.skill_description: Infrastructure as Code with Bicep Triggers on: **/*.bicep

actions.name: Use latest stable API versions for all resources
actions.description: Use latest stable API versions for all resources

actions.name: Set default values that are safe for test environments (use
actions.description: Set default values that are safe for test environments (use low-cost pricing tiers)

actions.name: Use parameters for settings that change between deployments
actions.description: Use parameters for settings that change between deployments

actions.name: Create resource dependencies through symbolic names (resourc
actions.description: Create resource dependencies through symbolic names (resourceA.id) not explicit dependsOn

actions.name: Use template expressions with uniqueString() to create meani
actions.description: Use template expressions with uniqueString() to create meaningful and unique resource names

actions.name: Include helpful // comments within your Bicep files to impro
actions.description: Include helpful // comments within your Bicep files to improve readability

rules.description: Always declare parameters at the top of files with @description decorators
Use @allowed decorator sparingly to avoid blocking valid deployments
Add prefixes to uniqueString() results since some resources don't allow names starting with numbers
Avoid excessive nesting of child resources
Never include secrets or keys in outputs

directives.description: When writing Bicep code, use lowerCamelCase for all names (variables, parameters, resources)
Use resource type descriptive symbolic names (e.g., 'storageAccount' not 'storageAccountName')
Avoid using 'name' in a symbolic name as it represents the resource, not the resource's name
Avoid distinguishing variables and parameters by the use of suffixes
Use descriptive @description decorators for all parameters
Specify minimum and maximum character length for naming parameters
Variables automatically infer type from the resolved value
Use variables to contain complex expressions instead of embedding them directly in resource properties
Use symbolic names for resource references instead of reference() or resourceId() functions
For accessing properties from other resources, use the 'existing' keyword instead of passing values through outputs
